title: Model invents plausible-sounding API that does not exist (async.LocalQueue)
description: During a multi-model fan-out code review (minicpm-v correctness perspective), the model emitted fix code referencing 'async.LocalQueue' — a class that does not exist in Python's standard library or any common asyncio ecosystem package. The model pattern-matched 'async queue for task tracking' to a plausible-sounding API rather than the correct asyncio.Queue or asyncio.Task set.
hallucination_signal: Small models (minicpm-v, 7B range) asked to produce fix code for async patterns will sometimes invent namespace-qualified class names that sound internally consistent. The invented API passes a quick syntax scan but fails at import time. Distinguishing markers: camelCase class in a snake_case module, no import visible in the generated code, the class is used but never defined.
good_example:
# Correct: use asyncio.Task set with done_callback cleanup
task = asyncio.create_task(self.stop())
self._tasks.append(task)
task.add_done_callback(lambda t: self._tasks.remove(t) if t in self._tasks else None)

detection_rule.context: class name not imported or defined in visible scope
detection_rule.message: Possible invented API — verify the module and class exist before accepting LLM-generated isinstance() checks
detection_rule.fix: Check the actual module's __all__ or source; replace with the real type or remove the isinstance guard

meta_note: This sample was itself generated by a code review job — the hallucination appeared in the review output, not the code under review. Meta-hallucination: LLM reviewing code hallucinates a fix that is itself broken.
